### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

All companies required to establish an Occupational Health and Safety Office/Department must record their occupational incidents, diseases, workforce numbers, and hours worked through the CSO’s online registry. The system then generates the Informe Anual, which must be downloaded and retained. CSO Registry Guide Instructional video for step-by-step guidance Your response will be reviewed by your connected clients.

Has your company submitted its occupational health and safety statistics to the Consejo de Salud Ocupacional (CSO) and obtained the official Informe Anual de Oficina o Departamento de Salud Ocupacional?
Yes
No - The company does not have any employees in Costa Rica so there is no requirement to register with the CSO.
No - The company has fewer than 10 permanent employees in Costa Rica and is not required or eligible to register with the CSO.

All companies registered with the Consejo de Salud Ocupacional (CSO) must complete the annual submission of occupational health and safety data through the CSO online system.\n\nUpon submission, the system generates the official **Informe Anual de Oficina o Departamento de Salud Ocupacional**, which must be downloaded and retained.
Please upload a copy of the official “Informe Anual de Oficina o Departamento de Salud Ocupacional” or the confirmation PDF generated by the CSO system upon submission.
